revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ideha-jinja]][[en:Dewa Shrine]][[ja:出羽神社]][[simple:Dewa Shrine]] {{Expand Japanese|出羽神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Dewa Shrine | native_name = 出羽神社 | image = Hagurosan Gohaiden 2006.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Dewa Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|38.702548|139.981354}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|http://www.dewasanzan.jp/publics/index/55/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Dewa Shrine'''|出羽神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Dewa Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Haguro shrine|lt=Haguro shrine|qid=Q126020344|1=Haguro shrine}} * {{ill|Beppyo shrine|lt=Beppyo Shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|ja|別表神社|mr|बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|simple|Beppyo shrine|zh|別表神社|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * Yamagata|wuu|鹤冈市|zh_classical|鶴岡市|zh_min_nan|Turuoka-chhī|zh_yue|鶴岡|zh|鶴岡市|qid=Q734416|1=Tsuruoka}} * {{ill|Q876032|ltq=Q876032|qid=Q876032|1=Dewa Province}} * {{ill|Tagawa district|lt=Tagawa district|ja|田川郡 (羽前国)|qid=Q11576762|1=Tagawa district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|38.702548|139.981354}} == official website (P856) == * http://www.dewasanzan.jp/publics/index/55/ ** ⵜⴰⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏⵜ|zh_classical|日語|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún-gí|zh_yue|日文|zh|日语|zhbooks|日语|zhvoyage|日语会话手册|zu|IsiJapani|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Hagurosan Gohaiden 2006.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182143 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Kokuhei Shōsha|lt=Kokuhei Shōsha|qid=Q134917281|1=Kokuhei Shōsha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == native label (P1705) == * {'text': '出羽神社', 'language': 'ja'} ** P1814: いではじんじゃ ** P2125: Idewa-jinja == VIAF cluster ID (P214) == * 255113277 == TripAdvisor ID (P3134) == * 1397273 == NDL Authority ID (P349) == * 00410973 == Commons category (P373) == * Ideha-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda, Haguro-san|lt=Five-storied Pagoda, Haguro-san|ja|羽黒山五重塔|qid=Q11610424|1=Five-storied Pagoda, Haguro-san}} * {{ill|Q7400109|ltq=Q7400109|qid=Q7400109|1=Saikan}} * {{ill|Q135194465|ltq=Q135194465|qid=Q135194465|1=Sanjin gousai-den, Ideha Shrine}} == image of interior (P5775) == * 2021-02-13 Dewa Sanzan Shrine.jpg == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '山形県鶴岡市羽黒町手向字羽黒山', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 出羽神社 == located in/on physical feature (P706) == * {{ill|Mount Haguro|lt=Mount Haguro|arz|جبل مونت هاجورو|ceb|Haguro Yama|en|Mount Haguro|eu|Haguro mendia|fa|کوه هاگورو|fr|Mont Haguro|id|Gunung Haguro|it|Monte Haguro|ja|羽黒山 (山形県)|lld|Dewa Shrine|pt|Monte Haguro|simple|Mount Haguro|zh|羽黑山|qid=Q3090765|1=Mount Haguro}} {{commons category|Category:Ideha-jinja}} [[EN:Dewa Shrine]] [[JA:出羽神社]] [[SIMPLE:Dewa Shrine]] {{wikidata link|Q11395802}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Shrine in Tsuruoka City, Yamagata Prefecture|Other uses|Dewa Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name=Dewa Shrine |Image = [[File:Hagurosan Gohaiden 2006.jpg|250px]]
Sanjin Gōsaiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=9|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|38|42|9.2|N|139|58|54.7|E}}}} |Location=Temiya {{ill|lt=Koaza|Q5327509|qid=Q5327509|lt=aza|1=koaza}} {{ill|lt=Mount Haguro (Yamagata Prefecture)|Q3090765|qid=Q3090765|lt=Mount Haguro|1=Mount Haguro}} 33, {{ill|lt=Haguro Town|Q3125724|qid=Q3125724|1=Haguro, Yamagata}}, {{ill|lt=Tsuruoka City|Q734416|qid=Q734416|1=Tsuruoka}}, {{ill|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|Q125863|qid=Q125863|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} | Latitude degrees = 38 | Latitude minutes = 42 | Latitude seconds = 9.2 | N (north latitude) or S (south latitude) = N | Longitude degrees = 139 | Longitude minutes = 58 | Longitude seconds = 54.7 | E (east longitude) or W (west longitude) = E | Map country code = JP |Map = Japan Yamagata |Enshrined deities=Ideha-no-Kami and {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Inakura Tamano Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}} |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor), {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Government shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kokuhei-shōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}, {{ill|lt=Beppyō Shrine|Q10898274|qid=Q10898274|1=Beppyo shrine}} |Founded= |Main hall= |Annual festival= }} '''Dewa Shrine''' (いではじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located on the summit of {{ill|lt=Mount Haguro (Yamagata Prefecture)|Q3090765|qid=Q3090765|lt=Mount Haguro|1=Mount Haguro}} in {{ill|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|Q125863|qid=Q125863|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}. The enshrined deities are Ideha-no-Kami and {{ill|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|qid=Q3080728|lt=Inakura Tamano Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine ranking|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines#Government shrines|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Kokuhei-shōsha|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. === Overview === {{ill|lt=Dewa Province|Q876032|qid=Q876032|1=Dewa Province}}’s southern region, the {{ill|lt=Shōnai region|Q11485804|qid=Q11485804|1=Shōnai region}} (the {{ill|lt=Sea of Japan|Q27092|qid=Q27092|1=Sea of Japan}} side of Yamagata Prefecture), contains {{ill|lt=Mount Gassan|Q3174863|qid=Q3174863|1=Mount Gassan}}, Mount Haguro, and {{ill|lt=Mount Yudono (Yamagata Prefecture)|Q3322191|qid=Q3322191|lt=Mount Yudono|1=Mount Yudono}}. These are collectively known as the {{ill|lt=Three Mountains of Dewa|Q1155726|qid=Q1155726|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} and have long been objects of {{ill|lt=mountain worship|Q93697439|qid=Q93697439|1=Mountain worship}}. Mount Haguro has served as one of the central sites of {{ill|lt=Shugendō|Q1483875|qid=Q1483875|1=Shugendō}}, which developed through the {{ill|lt=syncretism of Shinto and Buddhism|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|lt=fusion|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} of {{ill|lt=ancient Shinto|Q3096508|qid=Q3096508|1=Ko-Shintō}} with {{ill|lt=Buddhism|Q748|qid=Q748|1=Buddhism}} and other traditions.[http://www.dewasanzan.jp/publics/index/75/ Haguro School of Ancient Shugendō] Dewa Sanzan Shrine (accessed January 2, 2020) Mount Haguro has 2,446 stone steps, and within the mountain precincts there is also a {{ill|lt=five-story pagoda|Q11372713|qid=Q11372713|1=Five-storied pagoda}} (a {{ill|lt=National Treasure|Q1139795|qid=Q1139795|1=National Treasure (Japan)}}).[https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXMZO67152580Z01C20A2905W01/ No. 1: Dewa Sanzan Shrine (Yamagata Prefecture) — A serene and majestic space where Shinto and Buddhist deities dwell] {{ill|lt=Nihon Keizai Shimbun|Q1077694|qid=Q1077694|1=The Nikkei}}, December 12, 2020, Saturday morning supplement NIKKEI Plus 1 “Anything Ranking” (front page), accessed January 2, 2021 The Three Mountains of Dewa are said to symbolize the past (Mount Gassan), the present (Mount Haguro), and the future (Mount Yudono), and are respectively home to {{ill|lt=Gassan Shrine|Q11516052|qid=Q11516052|1=Gassan Shrine}}, Dewa Shrine, and {{ill|lt=Yudonosan Shrine|Q11563607|qid=Q11563607|1=Yudonosan Shrine}}. As a {{ill|lt=religious corporation|Q7311343|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}}, these three mountains are managed as a single corporation under the registered name “Gassan Shrine, Dewa Shrine, Yudonosan Shrine (Dewa Sanzan Shrine).” At Dewa Shrine on the summit of Mount Haguro stands the Sanjin Gōsaiden, which also houses the headquarters of the religious corporation. For cultural properties owned by the corporation, see “{{ill|lt=Three Mountains of Dewa#Cultural properties|Q1155726|qid=Q1155726|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}}.” === Gallery === Image:Hagurosan Syoro 2006.jpg|{{ill|lt=Bell tower|Q1363585|qid=Q1363585|1=Shōrō}} Image:Hagurosan Gohaiden kohai 2006.jpg|Sanjin Gōsaiden File:HaguroSan2.jpg|Sanjin Gōsaiden === Notes === {{Help:Footnotes}} {{reflist}} === Related literature === * Motohiko Yasu and Yoshihiko Umeda (eds. and supervisors), *Shinto Dictionary*, {{ill|lt=Jinja Shinpōsha|Q11590705|qid=Q11590705|1=Jinja Shinpō}}, 1968, pp. 40–41 * {{ill|lt=Eiji Shirai|Q11579387|qid=Q11579387|1=Eiji Shirai}} and Masanori Toki (eds.), *Shrine Dictionary*, {{ill|lt=Tōkyōdō Publishing|Q11524645|qid=Q11524645|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan}}, 1979, p. 40 * {{ill|lt=Masaaki Sugata|Q11619352|qid=Q11619352|1=Masaaki Sugata}}, *An “Encyclopedia” to Understand Japan’s Shrines*, {{ill|lt=Nihon Bungeisha|Q7034281|qid=Q7034281|1=Nihon Bungeisha}}, 1989, pp. 26–29 * {{ill|lt=Shunpei Ueyama|Q403579|qid=Q403579|1=Shunpei Ueyama}} et al., *Comprehensive Survey of Japanese “Shrines”*, {{ill|lt=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha|Q11501312|qid=Q11501312|1=Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha. "Rekishi Dokuhon" Henshūbu}}, 1992, pp. 64–65 * *The Book of Shinto*, {{ill|lt=Gakken|Q86741099|qid=Q86741099|lt=Gakken|1=Gakken Plus Co., Ltd.}}, 1992, p. 202 === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Three Mountains of Dewa|Q1155726|qid=Q1155726|1=Three Mountains of Dewa}} ** {{ill|lt=Gassan Shrine|Q11516052|qid=Q11516052|1=Gassan Shrine}} ** {{ill|lt=Yudonosan Shrine|Q11563607|qid=Q11563607|1=Yudonosan Shrine}} ** {{ill|lt=Iwanesawa Sanzan Shrine|Q11475213|qid=Q11475213|1=Iwaneaswa Sanzan Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Mizuki Hayama|Q24860872|qid=Q24860872|1=Mizuki Hayama}} — {{ill|lt=Enka|Q873478|qid=Q873478|1=Enka}} singer. For about six years, from after graduating high school until just before her debut in December 2015, she worked as a {{ill|lt=miko|Q1047139|qid=Q1047139|1=Miko}} at Dewa Sanzan Shrine. === External links === * [http://www.dewasanzan.jp/ Dewa Sanzan Shrine] (official website) {{Shinto sidebar}} {{Normdaten}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Dewa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Dewa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Dewa Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Dewa Province]] [[Category:Haguro Shrines]] [[Category:Shugendō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamagata Prefecture]] [[Category:鶴岡市の建築物]] [[Category:History of Tsuruoka]]
